reasoning: Zhang Lin holds the 800m freestyle long course world record (7:32.12), set during the super-suit era in 2009. The closest active swimmers (e.g., Hafnaoui, Short, Schwarz, and Wiffen) remain roughly 5-6 seconds away from this time, with the fastest recent times being over 7:37. A May 2025 Swimming World article described the record as 'out of reach.' Furthermore, the 2025 World Aquatics Championships already took place in July/August, and the short-term forecast horizon (Dec 2025 to Mar 2026) mainly coincides with the short-course season and early/off-peak long-course season, heavily reducing the opportunity for peak long-course performances. The historical 5-second gap to the world record is too large to realistically be closed in off-peak meets within the next three months. We cap the probability at the allowed 0.95 maximum and slightly decrease it for longer horizons to satisfy the increasing uncertainty constraint.
